channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: You don’t need an SSN to open a credit card: Scammers know that
item.description: More credit card issuers now approve applicants without a Social Security number, giving scammers new ways to commit identity theft using stolen data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The one thing scammers check before targeting you online
item.description: Most scams start with a simple search, not a hack. Your personal data is already publicly available on data broker sites and used to target you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to hide your sensitive info (for real) when using ChatGPT and other AI chatbots
item.description: Like many, I’ve never met a chatbot I trust completely. Not only do they have a propensity to hallucinate by making up facts, but you can never be sure what their parent companies do with the information you provide.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday release for April is a whopper
item.description: Windows admins are going to be busy this month, dealing with the largest Patch Tuesday cycle we can recall. The April release involves 165 updates and roughly 340 unique CVEs from Microsoft — including two zero-days, one of which is already being actively exploited in the wild.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Data centers are costing local governments billions
item.description: Tax benefits for hyperscalers and other data center operators are costing local administrations billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The stigma around AI in journalism may be easing, but trust is still fragile
item.description: I tend to write about AI from the perspective of the bleeding edge, looking at how journalists and media companies are using the technology to change the way they work, reach new audiences, and transform their organizations.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: AI isn’t built for all languages and cultures. There’s a push to fix that
item.description: Egyptian coder Assem Sabry has long wanted an AI model that represents his culture. The problem is he hasn’t been able to find one. “The AI industry in Egypt . . . doesn’t exist,” Sabry says. So he built his own: Horus, named after the ancient Egyptian god of the sky.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Astronomers measure the mind-blowing power and speed of black hole jets for the first time
item.description: Scientists for the first time have measured the instantaneous mind-blowing power of jets blasting from a black hole.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft to cut Windows 365 price for SMBs
item.description: Microsoft will cut the price of Windows 365 subscriptions for small and mid-sized businesses by 20% next month, though analysts expect little impact on uptake of the Desktop-as-a-Service (DaaS) platform.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Roblox adds age-based accounts for kids and teens
item.description: Roblox is rolling out Kids and Select account types in early June, building separate environments for different age groups with built-in safety controls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tap into the AI APIs of Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge
item.description: With every passing year, local AI models get smaller, more efficient, and more comparable in power with their higher-end, cloud-hosted counterparts. You can run many of the same inference jobs on your own hardware, without needing an internet connection or even a particularly powerful GPU.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Satellite backhaul service Globalstar has a new, rich owner amid challenging market conditions
item.description: Globalstar, a mobile satellite services (MSS) operator in which Apple has a 20% stake, on Tuesday announced a merger agreement with Amazon, which, pending regulatory approval, could soon bring direct to device services (D2D) services to Leo, the latter’s low Earth orbit satellite network.
item.dc:creator: Network World
